intent: A project management platform designed to automate task prioritization using an AI-driven engine that analyzes historical team velocity and resource capacity. It differentiates itself by automatically rescheduling dependencies when a bottleneck is detected in real-time, shifting focus from manual tracking to predictive workflow management.

entities.name: ProjectMetricSnapshot
entities.description: An immutable record of historical team velocity, sprint cycle times, and resource utilization levels captured from integrated external platforms.

entities.name: DependencyGraphNode
entities.description: A representation of a task or sub-task including its temporal constraints, resource requirements, and linked successor/predecessor relationships.

entities.name: BottleneckHeuristicEngine
entities.description: A stateful compute module that continuously scans the DependencyGraphNode cluster to identify constraint violations and projected drift from the critical path.

entities.name: AdaptiveResourceAllocator
entities.description: An intelligent controller that maps AvailableCapacity units against task priorities to automatically suggest or commit re-assignments.

entities.name: SyncAdapterService
entities.description: A middleware layer facilitating normalized bi-directional data flow between external tool APIs and the internal domain model.

entities.name: AccessControlPolicy
entities.description: A centralized authorization schema defining the CRUD permissions for entities based on user roles and workspace scope.

entities.name: WorkstreamExecutionLog
entities.description: An audit trail tracking every automated rescheduling event and manual user override for compliance and transparency.

architecture.description: The system employs an event-driven architecture where the SyncAdapterService ingests disparate data from external sources and persists it into the central database. The BottleneckHeuristicEngine continuously evaluates the current state against historical baselines stored in ProjectMetricSnapshot. Upon detecting a scheduling conflict, the engine updates the DependencyGraphNode structure. The AdaptiveResourceAllocator then triggers a reconciliation loop that recalculates timelines, pushes updates back to external platforms via the SyncAdapterService, and logs all changes in the WorkstreamExecutionLog. Role-based security via AccessControlPolicy is enforced at every API boundary to ensure data privacy during these automated workflows.

endpoints.summary: Create a new project metric snapshot
Update an existing dependency graph node
Trigger a resource allocation reconciliation loop
Force ingest data from external sources via SyncAdapter

rules.description: Ensure immutability of historical metric data.
rules.logic: ProjectMetricSnapshot records cannot be updated or deleted once persisted; only insertion of new snapshots is permitted.
New relationships between DependencyGraphNodes must be verified to not create circular dependencies before persisting the update.

rules.description: Enforce authorization boundary on external data sync.
rules.logic: Verify AccessControlPolicy permissions for the specific workspace before allowing SyncAdapterService to write or push updates to external APIs.

rules.description: Audit trail generation for automated reassignments.
rules.logic: Every state transition initiated by the AdaptiveResourceAllocator must generate an entry in the WorkstreamExecutionLog containing the previous state, new state, and reason code.
Projected drift from the critical path must exceed the pre-defined project tolerance defined in ProjectMetricSnapshot before the BottleneckHeuristicEngine triggers a node update.
